beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make a proper espresso at home.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian starts his Monday with full energy. He's particular about coffee—espresso at home, never drip—so this sets his morning tone before heading to work.

beats.activity: Drive from Magnolia to Kirkland Metro depot.
beats.location_name: King County Metro Transit Center (Kirkland, WA)
beats.reasoning: Across-town commute from Magnolia to Kirkland; 50 min budgeted for morning traffic on a sunny Monday.

beats.activity: Work shift: operate Metro bus routes; note Light Rail closure 9am-3pm won't affect his route today.
beats.location_name: King County Metro Transit Center (Kirkland, WA)
beats.reasoning: Sebastian's primary work shift. The Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District is irrelevant to his Kirkland-based bus routes, so he carries on normally.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and coffee at a local spot near the depot.
beats.reasoning: Quick, quiet lunch during his midday break. Whole Foods gives him options for a quality meal without fuss.

beats.activity: Return to work: afternoon shift operating Metro bus routes.
beats.location_name: King County Metro Transit Center (Kirkland, WA)
beats.reasoning: Continuation of his work day. Afternoon shift wraps up in time for leisure before evening.

beats.activity: Commute home from Kirkland to Magnolia.
beats.reasoning: Drive back home after work shift. 45 min budgeted for late-afternoon traffic crossing from Kirkland to Magnolia.

beats.activity: Quick freshen-up and snack at home; change into casual clothes.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian returns home and takes a moment to decompress before heading out. He's introverted and values quiet time to reset.

beats.activity: Drive downtown to Pike Place Market area for an early dinner and social time.
beats.reasoning: Heads downtown on this sunny evening. The USA vs Wales FIFA match aired at 11am, but Sebastian missed it at work—he's not bothered by missing it, preferring to spend his evening socializing over a meal.

beats.activity: Dinner and drinks with a close friend at a restaurant near Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian values time with close friends over crowds. He meets a friend for an unhurried dinner on this beautiful Monday evening, enjoying the social connection.
Return home from downtown after a pleasant evening. 45 min budgeted for evening traffic.

beats.activity: Relax at home: read, listen to music, or journal; wind down for the night.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian returns home and settles in for a quiet evening of low-key leisure, maintaining his introverted rhythm before bed.
Full night's rest at home after a balanced work day and social evening. Sleep ends well before dawn of the next day.

diary: Started the day right with a proper espresso at home—nothing beats that ritual before heading out. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, and the morning shift was steady; barely noticed the Light Rail closure since I'm on the bus routes anyway. Grabbed a sandwich at Whole Foods during lunch, nothing fancy but it hit the spot. Afternoon flew by, and honestly, I was ready to be done by 2:30. Freshened up at home and decided to head downtown on this gorgeous afternoon—too nice to waste. Met up with an old friend at Matt's near Pike Place Market for dinner and drinks. We caught some of the World Cup match on the bar TV, USA playing Wales. The whole downtown felt electric with folks gathering to watch. Nice to disconnect from the route schedules and just sit with good company. Drove back to Magnolia as the sun was getting golden, feeling more settled than I have in weeks.
thought_process: Today felt like a natural rhythm. I kept my shift routine tight because I knew I wanted flexibility afterward—the Light Rail closure didn't impact my routes, which was lucky. The World Cup buzz downtown pulled me in; normally I'd just head home, but the weather and daylight made it feel worth the extra drive. My friend texted earlier in the week, so I'd already planned it, but seeing all those people gathering at bars reminded me how much I need those moments of connection outside the cab. My mood shifted around lunchtime from autopilot to something more present. By evening, I felt genuinely recharged rather than just tired from work.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
